<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by `cargo xtask gen-docs` тАФ do not edit manually -->

Anodizer uses `.anodizer.yaml` (or `.anodizer.toml`) in your project root.

## Top-Level Fields

| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `after` | HooksConfig | тАФ | Hooks run after the release pipeline completes. |
| `announce` | AnnounceConfig | тАФ | Announcement configuration (Slack, Discord, email, etc.). |
| `appimages` | list of AppImageConfig | `[]` | AppImage configurations. Each entry bundles a built Linux binary plus its desktop integration into a single self-contained `.AppImage` via linuxdeploy. |
| `artifactories` | list of ArtifactoryConfig | тАФ | Artifactory upload configurations. |
| `attestations` | AttestationConfig | тАФ | SLSA build-provenance / attestation configuration for binaries and archives. In the default `subjects` mode, anodizer writes a subjects manifest for `actions/attest-build-provenance`; in `emit` mode it generates and signs a self-contained in-toto SLSA provenance statement. When omitted (or `enabled: false`), the attestation stage is a no-op. |
| `aur_sources` | list of AurSourceConfig | тАФ | AUR source package publishing configurations (source-only PKGBUILD, not -bin). |
| `before` | HooksConfig | тАФ | Hooks run before the release pipeline starts. |
| `before_publish` | HooksConfig | тАФ | Hooks run after build/archive/sign/sbom/checksum complete but immediately before the publish phase dispatches any publisher.<br><br>Use cases: smoke-test artifacts against the staged dist tree, run external validators (antivirus, vulnerability scanners), stage external state, or abort the release before any publisher writes to a registry.<br><br>A non-zero exit code from any hook aborts the release before publish runs. Hooks fire in declared order. Use `--skip=before-publish` to bypass. |
| `binary_signs` | list of SignConfig | `[]` | Binary-specific signing configs (same shape as `signs` but only for binary artifacts). The `artifacts` field on each entry is constrained at parse time to `binary` / `none` (or omitted) тАФ a broader filter on `binary_signs` would silently match nothing because the loop only iterates Binary artifacts. Constraint lives in `deserialize_binary_signs`. |
| `changelog` | ChangelogConfig | тАФ | Changelog generation configuration. |
| `cloudsmiths` | list of CloudSmithConfig | тАФ | CloudSmith publisher configurations. |
| `crates` | list of CrateConfig | `[]` | List of crates in this project. |
| `defaults` | Defaults | тАФ | Default values applied to all crates unless overridden. |
| `dist` | string | `./dist` | Output directory for build artifacts (default: ./dist). |
| `docker_signs` | list of DockerSignConfig | тАФ | Docker image signing configurations. |
| `dockerhub` | list of DockerHubConfig | тАФ | DockerHub description sync configurations. |
| `env` | list of string | тАФ | Environment variables available to all template expressions.<br><br>List of `KEY=VALUE` strings: `env: ["MY_VAR=hello", "DEPLOY_ENV=staging"]`. Order is preserved so chained env applications (sign + sbom + notarize) see entries in declared order. Values are rendered through the template engine before being set, so expressions like `{{ Tag }}` or `{{ Date }}` are expanded. |
| `env_files` | EnvFilesConfig | тАФ | Environment file configuration. Accepts either: - A list of `.env` file paths: `[".env", ".release.env"]` - A struct with token file paths: `{ github_token: "~/.config/goreleaser/github_token" }` |
| `force_token` | ForceTokenKind | тАФ | Force a specific token type for authentication. When set, overrides automatic token detection from environment variables. |
| `gemfury` | list of GemFuryConfig | тАФ | GemFury (fury.io) deb/rpm/apk publishing configurations. Mirrors The `gemfury:` block. The legacy spelling `furies:` is accepted via serde alias; a one-time deprecation warning is emitted by `warn_on_legacy_furies_alias`. |
| `git` | GitConfig | тАФ | Git-level tag discovery and sorting settings. |
| `gitea_urls` | GiteaUrlsConfig | тАФ | Custom Gitea API/download URLs for self-hosted Gitea installations. |
| `github_urls` | GitHubUrlsConfig | тАФ | Custom GitHub API/upload/download URLs for GitHub Enterprise installations. |
| `gitlab_urls` | GitLabUrlsConfig | тАФ | Custom GitLab API/download URLs for self-hosted GitLab installations. |
| `homebrew_casks` | list of HomebrewCaskConfig | тАФ | Top-level Homebrew Cask configurations. `homebrew_casks` is a top-level array with its own repository, commit_author, directory, skip_upload, hooks, dependencies, conflicts, completions, manpages, structured uninstall/zap, etc. |
| `homebrew_cores` | list of HomebrewCoreConfig | тАФ | homebrew-core formula-bump configurations. One entry per formula. Bumps an existing formula in `Homebrew/homebrew-core` (or a formula repository override) via the GitHub API and opens a pull request. The `homebrew_cores:` block. |
| `includes` | list of IncludeSpec | тАФ | Additional config files to merge into this config. Supports plain string paths, `from_file:` for structured file paths, and `from_url:` for fetching configs from URLs with optional headers. |
| `install_scripts` | list of InstallScriptConfig | `[]` | `curl \| sh` installer-script configurations. Each entry emits a deterministic POSIX `install.sh` release asset that detects the host OS + arch, downloads and sha256-verifies the matching archive, and installs the binary. |
| `makeselfs` | list of MakeselfConfig | `[]` | Makeself self-extracting archive configurations. |
| `mcp` | McpConfig | `{"name":null,"title":null,"description":null,"homepage":null,"packages":[],"transports":[],"skip":null,"repository":{"url":"","source":"","id":"","subfolder":""},"auth":{"type":"none"},"registry":null,"if":null,"retain_on_rollback":null}` | MCP (Model Context Protocol) server registry publishing configuration. When `name` is empty (the default), the publisher is skipped. The `mcp:` publisher block. |
| `metadata` | MetadataConfig | тАФ | Project metadata configuration (applied to metadata.json output files). |
| `milestones` | list of MilestoneConfig | тАФ | Milestone closing configurations. |
| `monorepo` | MonorepoConfig | тАФ | Monorepo configuration. When configured, tag discovery filters by tag_prefix and the working directory is scoped to dir. |
| `nightly` | NightlyConfig | тАФ | Nightly release configuration. |
| `notarize` | NotarizeConfig | тАФ | macOS code signing and notarization configuration. |
| `npms` | list of NpmConfig | тАФ | NPM package registry publishing configurations. One entry per published package. In the default `optional-deps` mode anodizer emits npm's native per-platform packages (biome / git-cliff pattern); in `postinstall` mode it emits a download shim (the `npms:` parity). |
| `on_error` | HooksConfig | тАФ | Hooks run when the release pipeline fails at ANY stage (build, sign, publish, ...), after the failure policy (rollback / hold) has executed, so `{{ .RolledBack }}` reflects the taken path.<br><br>Notification / cleanup hooks: a hook's own failure is logged as a warning and never masks the pipeline error. The failure context is exposed both as template vars (`{{ .Error }}`, `{{ .RolledBack }}`) and as `ANODIZER_*` env vars (`ANODIZER_ERROR`, `ANODIZER_ROLLED_BACK`, `ANODIZER_VERSION`, `ANODIZER_TAG`) so hooks can consume the error text without shell interpolation.<br><br>```yaml on_error: hooks: - cmd: ./notify-release-failed.sh ``` |
| `partial` | PartialConfig | тАФ | Partial/split build configuration for fan-out CI pipelines. |
| `preflight` | PreflightConfig | `{"strict":false}` | Pre-publish preflight tuning. `preflight.strict: true` promotes indeterminate probe outcomes (5xx / rate-limit / network failure / undeterminable permissions) from warnings to hard blockers. The probes themselves always run read-only before any publisher mutates a registry; the default (lenient) behavior needs no config. |
| `project_name` | string | тАФ | Human-readable project name used in templates and release titles. |
| `publishers` | list of PublisherConfig | тАФ | Generic artifact publisher configurations. |
| `pypis` | list of PypiConfig | тАФ | PyPI publishing configurations. One entry per published project. Emits native `py3-none-<platform>` binary wheels from the built binaries (plus an optional `maturin sdist`) and uploads them via PyPI's legacy (twine-protocol) upload API. The `pypis:` block. |
| `release` | ReleaseConfig | тАФ | GitHub release configuration shared by all crates. |
| `report_sizes` | bool | тАФ | When true, log artifact file sizes after building. |
| `retry` | RetryConfig | тАФ | Top-level retry configuration applied to network-bound operations (announcers, git providers, HTTP uploads, docker pipes). When omitted, `RetryConfig::default()` is used (10 attempts, 10s base, 5m cap тАФ the project-level retry policy). |
| `sboms` | list of SbomConfig | `[]` | Software bill of materials (SBOM) generation configurations. |
| `schemastore` | SchemastoreConfig | `{"repository":null,"commit_author":null,"versioned":null,"skip":null,"if":null,"schemas":[],"retain_on_rollback":null}` | SchemaStore publisher. Registers the project's JSON Schema(s) on SchemaStore at release time. When `schemas` is empty (the default), the publisher is skipped. The `schemastore:` publisher block. |
| `signs` | list of SignConfig | `[]` | Signing configurations for binaries, archives, and checksums. |
| `snapshot` | SnapshotConfig | тАФ | Snapshot release configuration (local/non-tag builds). |
| `source` | SourceConfig | тАФ | Source archive configuration. |
| `srpms` | SrpmConfig | тАФ | Source RPM configuration. Renamed from `srpm:` (singular) for spelling parity with `Defaults.srpms` and the rest of the plural-name packaging fields. The `srpm:` spelling is still accepted via serde alias for back-compat. |
| `tag` | TagConfig | тАФ | Automatic semantic version tagging configuration. |
| `template_files` | list of TemplateFileConfig | тАФ | Template files to render and include as release artifacts. File contents are processed through the template engine. |
| `uploads` | list of UploadConfig | тАФ | Generic HTTP upload configurations. |
| `upx` | list of UpxConfig | `[]` | UPX binary compression configurations. |
| `variables` | map | тАФ | Custom template variables accessible as `{{ Var.<key> }}` in templates. Provides a way to define reusable values, especially useful with config includes.<br><br>Stored as a `BTreeMap` so rendering iterates in deterministic (sorted) key order тАФ without this guarantee, a value that references another variable (`b: "{{ Var.a }}_v2"`) could render before its dependency on a different process / host. The current resolver is single-pass (one render per value), so cross-variable references only resolve when the referenced key sorts earlier. |
| `verify_release` | VerifyReleaseConfig | `{"enabled":false,"assert_assets":true,"assert_landing":true,"install_smoke":null}` | Opt-in post-release verification gate. Runs LAST (after the release is created and every publisher has run) and REPORTS post-publish defects тАФ missing assets, failed install smoke-tests, glibc-ceiling violations. Because it runs after the irreversible publish, a failure exits non-zero to flag CI but never undoes the release. Off unless `verify_release.enabled: true`. |
| `version` | integer | тАФ | Schema version. Currently supports 1 (implicit default) and 2. |
| `version_files` | list of string | тАФ | Repo-committed files that embed the release version outside `Cargo.toml` (e.g. a Helm `Chart.yaml`, an install doc, a README badge), given as repo-root-relative path strings. At `tag` time each listed file has its occurrences of the old version rewritten to the new version тАФ both the bare (`0.1.0`) and `v`-prefixed (`v0.1.0`) forms, word-boundary anchored тАФ and is staged into the same bump commit as `Cargo.toml` / `Cargo.lock`, so these files never drift from the tag.<br><br>```yaml version_files: - charts/cfgd/Chart.yaml - docs/installation.md ``` |
| `workspaces` | list of WorkspaceConfig | тАФ | Independent workspace roots in a monorepo. |

## `after`
Top-level lifecycle hooks for `before` and `after` blocks. Each block carries a list of hook commands that run around the entire pipeline (not individual stages).

The canonical key is `hooks:` for both `before:` and `after:` to the conventional spelling. The `post:` spelling is accepted as a serde alias on `hooks` for back-compat with the previous anodizer spelling; users with `after: { post: [...] }` keep working and a deprecation warning is logged when both spellings appear in the same block (see `HooksConfig::merge_hook_aliases`).
| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `hooks` | list of HookEntry | тАФ | Commands to run when the block fires. The wire format accepts either `hooks:` (canonical) or the legacy `post:` spelling; both fold into this field at parse time. |
| `post` | list of HookEntry | тАФ | Legacy alias for `hooks:` (anodizer pre-v0.4). Always `None` after parsing тАФ `merge_hook_aliases` collapses it into `hooks`. Present on the struct only because `Deserialize` writes through it before the fold step. |

## `announce`
Announce-stage integrations.

Message bodies are secret-redacted before send: known secret env values are masked (a real token becomes `$NAME`). Redaction is on by default; `anodizer notify --allow-secrets` opts a single send out for a trusted private channel, while anodizer's own log output stays redacted regardless.
| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `bluesky` | BlueskyAnnounce | тАФ | Bluesky announcement configuration. |
| `deadline` | HumanDuration | тАФ | Overall wall-clock deadline for the announce stage (e.g. `"90s"`, `"2m"`). Optional тАФ defaults to `DEFAULT_ANNOUNCE_DEADLINE` (90s).<br><br>Announcers run concurrently; any still running when this deadline elapses is abandoned with a warning rather than awaited. This bounds the stage so unreachable channels cannot accumulate into a hang that trips the pipeline timeout *after* publishers already crossed one-way doors. Raise it only if a slow-but-reachable channel legitimately needs longer. |
| `discord` | DiscordAnnounce | тАФ | Discord announcement configuration. |
| `discourse` | DiscourseAnnounce | тАФ | Discourse announcement configuration. |
| `email` | EmailAnnounce | тАФ | Email announcement configuration. accepts the historical `smtp:` key as an alias because the field was renamed `smtp:` -> `email:` in v1.21+ and kept the alias for migration. Keeping the alias avoids forcing a re-yaml of legacy configs. |
| `gate_on` | AnnounceGate | `required_publishers` | Selects when AnnounceStage runs vs. skips based on the `PublishReport` written by PublishStage/BlobStage. Default is `required_publishers` (announce only if every required publisher succeeded). See `AnnounceGate` for the other variants. |
| `if` | string | тАФ | Template-conditional gate: when the rendered result is falsy (`"false"` / `"0"` / `"no"` / empty), the entire announce stage is skipped. Render failure hard-errors. The `announce.if:`. Distinct from `skip:` (always-skip predicate) тАФ both surfaces are documented. |
| `linkedin` | LinkedInAnnounce | тАФ | LinkedIn announcement configuration. |
| `mastodon` | MastodonAnnounce | тАФ | Mastodon announcement configuration. |
| `mattermost` | MattermostAnnounce | тАФ | Mattermost announcement configuration. |
| `opencollective` | OpenCollectiveAnnounce | тАФ | OpenCollective announcement configuration. |
| `reddit` | RedditAnnounce | тАФ | Reddit announcement configuration. |
| `skip` | StringOrBool | тАФ | Template-conditional skip: if rendered to "true", skip the entire announce stage. |
| `slack` | SlackAnnounce | тАФ | Slack announcement configuration. |
| `teams` | TeamsAnnounce | тАФ | Microsoft Teams announcement configuration. |
| `telegram` | TelegramAnnounce | тАФ | Telegram announcement configuration. |
| `twitter` | TwitterAnnounce | тАФ | Twitter/X announcement configuration. |
| `webhook` | WebhookConfig | тАФ | Generic webhook announcement configuration. |

## `appimages`
AppImage packaging configuration.

Drives the [AppImage](https://appimage.org/) stage, which bundles a built Linux binary plus its desktop integration (a `.desktop` entry + icon) into a single self-contained, runnable `.AppImage` file via [`linuxdeploy`](https://github.com/linuxdeploy/linuxdeploy)'s `appimage` output plugin. One `.AppImage` is produced per matching Linux target so a multi-arch build yields distinct, non-colliding outputs.

YAML: ```yaml appimages: - id: helix ids: [helix-bin] desktop: contrib/Helix.desktop icon: contrib/helix.png appdir_extra: - src: runtime/ dst: usr/lib/helix/runtime update_information: "gh-releases-zsync|helix-editor|helix|latest|helix-*.AppImage.zsync" runtime_harvest: command: "{{ ArtifactPath }} --populate-runtime {{ HarvestDir }}" dir: runtime/ ```
| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `appdir_extra` | list of AppImageExtra | тАФ | Extra files / directories copied into the AppDir before linuxdeploy runs (e.g. a harvested `runtime/` tree). Each entry's `dst` is interpreted relative to the AppDir root. |
| `arch` | list of string | тАФ | Target architecture filter. When omitted, every architecture in the build matrix produces its own `.AppImage`. |
| `desktop` | string | тАФ | Path to the `.desktop` entry file (template). Required тАФ linuxdeploy will not assemble an AppImage without a desktop file. |
| `extra_args` | list of string | тАФ | Extra arguments appended to the linuxdeploy command line. |
| `filename` | string | тАФ | Output filename template (default includes project, version, os, arch). The `.AppImage` extension is appended automatically when absent. |
| `icon` | string | тАФ | Path to the application icon (template). Required. |
| `id` | string | тАФ | Unique identifier for this AppImage config (default: "default"). |
| `ids` | list of string | тАФ | Build IDs filter: only bundle binaries whose `id` is in this list. When omitted, every Linux binary in the build matrix is eligible. |
| `name` | string | тАФ | Application name passed to linuxdeploy via the `APP` env var and used as the AppDir basename. Defaults to the project name. |
| `os` | list of string | тАФ | Target OS filter (default: ["linux"]). AppImage is a Linux-only format. |
| `runtime_harvest` | RuntimeHarvest | тАФ | Runtime-asset harvest hook: run the freshly-built binary ONCE on the host to populate a directory, then bundle that directory into the AppDir. The harvested data is architecture-independent (grammars, themes, queries), so it is produced once on the host-native binary and reused for every target's AppImage. |
| `skip` | StringOrBool | тАФ | Skip this config. Accepts bool or template string. |
| `update_information` | string | тАФ | zsync delta-update metadata embedded in the AppImage, passed to linuxdeploy via the `UPDATE_INFORMATION` env var. When omitted, the AppImage carries no update information and `UPDATE_INFORMATION` is left unset (matching linuxdeploy's default). |

## `artifactories`
Artifactory upload configuration. Uploads artifacts to JFrog Artifactory repositories.
| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `checksum` | bool | тАФ | Include checksums in uploaded artifacts. |
| `checksum_header` | string | тАФ | Header name used for checksum verification (e.g. `X-Checksum-Sha256`). |
| `client_x509_cert` | string | тАФ | Path to client X.509 certificate for mTLS authentication. |
| `client_x509_key` | string | тАФ | Path to client X.509 private key for mTLS authentication. |
| `custom_artifact_name` | bool | тАФ | Use custom artifact naming instead of default. |
| `custom_headers` | map | тАФ | Custom HTTP headers sent with each upload request. |
| `deb_architecture` | string | тАФ | Override the Debian architecture for `.deb` uploads (`;deb.architecture=`). When unset (the default), the architecture is derived from each artifact's build target (`x86_64` тЖТ `amd64`, `aarch64` тЖТ `arm64`, `armv7` тЖТ `armhf`, `i686` тЖТ `i386`, тАж), so it never needs to be set by hand. Set this only to force a value for an artifact whose target can't be mapped. Ignored for non-`.deb` artifacts. |
| `deb_components` | list of string | тАФ | Debian repository component(s) for `.deb` uploads, written into the `;deb.component=` matrix param. Defaults to `["main"]` when unset. Multiple components are emitted comma-separated. Ignored for non-`.deb` artifacts. |
| `deb_distributions` | list of string | тАФ | Debian repository distribution(s) for `.deb` uploads, written into the Artifactory `;deb.distribution=` upload matrix param so apt can index the package. Defaults to `["stable"]` when unset. A multi-element list is emitted as Artifactory's comma-separated form (`deb.distribution=bookworm,bullseye`), publishing the same `.deb` into several distributions at once. Ignored for non-`.deb` artifacts. |
| `exclude` | list of string | тАФ | Glob patterns matched against each artifact's file name; anodizer drops any artifact whose name matches at least one glob from THIS Artifactory target only. Use it to keep heavy sidecars (checksums, signatures, SBOMs) off a given repository while archives still upload. Composes with `ids:` and `exts:` (all filters apply). `None`/empty keeps everything.<br><br>```yaml artifactories: - target: "https://repo.example.com/{{ .ProjectName }}/{{ .Tag }}/{{ .ArtifactName }}" exclude: ["*.sha256", "*.sig", "*.cdx.json"] ``` |
| `extra_files` | list of ExtraFileSpec | тАФ | Extra files to upload alongside build artifacts. |
| `extra_files_only` | bool | тАФ | When true, upload only extra_files (skip normal artifacts). |
| `exts` | list of string | тАФ | File extension filter: only upload artifacts matching these extensions. |
| `ids` | list of string | тАФ | Build IDs filter: only upload artifacts from builds whose `id` is in this list. |
| `if` | string | тАФ | Template-conditional gate: when the rendered result is falsy (`"false"` / `"0"` / `"no"` / empty), the artifactory publisher is skipped. Render failure hard-errors. The `artifactories[].if:`. |
| `meta` | bool | тАФ | Include metadata artifacts in uploaded artifacts. |
| `method` | string | тАФ | HTTP method to use for uploads (default: "PUT"). |
| `mode` | string | тАФ | Upload mode: "archive" (upload archives) or "binary" (upload binaries). |
| `name` | string | тАФ | Human-readable name for this publisher (used in logs). |
| `overwrite` | bool | тАФ | Re-upload an artifact even when an identical one already exists at the target path (default: `false`).<br><br>With the default, a re-run that finds the same version's artifact already uploaded with a matching SHA-256 records an idempotent SKIP rather than re-PUTting it тАФ so re-running a partially-failed release is safe. A path that already holds a *different* artifact for the same version still hard-errors (immutable-version drift) unless `overwrite` is set. With `overwrite: true`, every artifact is PUT unconditionally (Artifactory replaces the stored copy), restoring blind-overwrite behaviour for repos configured to allow it. |
| `password` | string | тАФ | Artifactory password or API key (or env var reference). |
| `required` | bool | тАФ | Override whether this publisher failing should fail the overall release.<br><br>Default: `false` тАФ a failure here is logged but does not abort the release. Set to `true` to fail the release on any error. |
| `retain_on_rollback` | bool | тАФ | When `true`, a triggered rollback leaves this publisher's work in place rather than attempting to undo it. Default `false`. |
| `signature` | bool | тАФ | Include signatures in uploaded artifacts. |
| `skip` | StringOrBool | тАФ | Template-conditional skip: if rendered result is `"true"`, skip this publisher. |
| `target` | string | тАФ | Target URL template for uploads (supports template variables). |
| `trusted_certificates` | string | тАФ | PEM-encoded trusted CA certificates for TLS verification. Appended to the system certificate pool. |
| `username` | string | тАФ | Artifactory username for authentication. |

## `attestations`
SLSA build-provenance / attestation configuration for binaries and archives.

Two modes select how anodizer participates in attestation:

- `AttestationMode::Subjects` (the default) emits a **subjects manifest** (`dist/attestation-subjects.json`) that `anodizer-action` feeds to GitHub's `actions/attest-build-provenance`. anodizer does NOT mint a GitHub-trusted attestation itself in this mode тАФ the Action's OIDC identity does. This is the path fd / biome / gping use. - `AttestationMode::Emit` generates a self-contained in-toto v1 statement carrying an SLSA provenance v1 predicate over the selected artifacts, writes it as a release asset (`attestation.intoto.jsonl`), and lets the existing `signs:` stage sign it (keyed, not OIDC). This is for users who can't run the Action (the `--with-provenance` toggle).

YAML: ```yaml attestations: enabled: true mode: subjects          # or: emit ; default = subjects artifacts: [archive, binary, checksum] ```
| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `artifacts` | list of AttestationArtifactKind | тАФ | Which produced-artifact kinds to attest. Each entry selects a KIND (`archive`, `binary`, `checksum`); the concrete subject set (filenames + sha256) is DERIVED from the artifacts anodizer already produced.<br><br>Defaults to `[archive, binary, checksum]` when omitted. |
| `enabled` | bool | `false` | Enable attestation. When false (the default), the stage is a no-op. |
| `mode` | AttestationMode | тАФ | Participation mode: `subjects` (default) writes a manifest for `actions/attest-build-provenance`; `emit` generates and signs an in-toto SLSA provenance statement as a release asset. |
| `skip` | StringOrBool | тАФ | Skip the attestation stage. Accepts a bool or a template string. |

## `aur_sources`
| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `amd64_variant` | Amd64Variant | тАФ | `x86_64` micro-architecture variant тАФ `v1` (baseline), `v2`, `v3` (AVX2), or `v4`. Constrained to a typed enum because AUR source pkgs build from the upstream tarball (no binary artifacts to filter), so the value's only role is as the `Amd64` template var consumed by `prepare:` / `build:` / `package:` script bodies тАФ typos must fail at parse time, not silently render an invalid string into the PKGBUILD. When unset, defaults to `v1` at template-render time. |
| `arches` | list of string | тАФ | Explicit architecture list (default: auto-detect from artifacts). |
| `backup` | list of string | тАФ | Backup files to preserve on upgrade. |
| `build` | string | тАФ | Custom `build()` function body for PKGBUILD. |
| `commit_author` | CommitAuthorConfig | тАФ | Commit author with optional signing. |
| `commit_msg_template` | string | тАФ | Custom commit message template. |
| `conflicts` | list of string | тАФ | Packages this PKGBUILD conflicts with. |
| `contributors` | list of string | тАФ | Contributors listed in PKGBUILD comments. |
| `depends` | list of string | тАФ | Runtime dependencies. |
| `description` | string | тАФ | Short description of the package. |
| `directory` | string | тАФ | Subdirectory in the git repo for committed files. |
| `git_ssh_command` | string | тАФ | Custom SSH command for git operations. |
| `git_url` | string | тАФ | AUR SSH git URL override. Defaults to `ssh://aur@aur.archlinux.org/<package>.git`, derived from the resolved package name; set this only for a non-standard endpoint. |
| `homepage` | string | тАФ | Project homepage URL. |
| `ids` | list of string | тАФ | Build IDs filter. |
| `if` | string | тАФ | Template-conditional gate: when the rendered result is falsy (`"false"` / `"0"` / `"no"` / empty), the AUR source config is skipped. Render failure hard-errors. The `aur_sources[].if:`. |
| `install` | string | тАФ | Content for a .install file (post-install/pre-remove scripts). |
| `license` | string | тАФ | SPDX license identifier. |
| `maintainers` | list of string | тАФ | PKGBUILD maintainer entries. |
| `makedepends` | list of string | тАФ | Build-time dependencies (source packages need these). |
| `name` | string | тАФ | Override the package name (default: crate name, no -bin suffix). |
| `optdepends` | list of string | тАФ | Optional dependencies. |
| `package` | string | тАФ | Custom `package()` function body for PKGBUILD. |
| `prepare` | string | тАФ | Custom `prepare()` function body for PKGBUILD. |
| `private_key` | string | тАФ | Path to SSH private key file. |
| `provides` | list of string | тАФ | Packages this PKGBUILD provides. |
| `rel` | string | тАФ | Package release number (default: "1"). |
| `required` | bool | тАФ | Override whether this publisher failing should fail the overall release.<br><br>Default: `false` тАФ a failure here is logged but does not abort the release. Set to `true` to fail the release on any error. |
| `retain_on_rollback` | bool | тАФ | When `true`, a triggered rollback leaves this publisher's work in place rather than attempting to undo it. Default `false`. |
| `skip` | StringOrBool | тАФ | Skip this config. Accepts the legacy `disable:` spelling via serde alias for back-compat. |
| `skip_upload` | StringOrBool | тАФ | Skip publishing. `"true"` always skips; `"auto"` skips for prereleases. |
| `url_template` | string | тАФ | Custom URL template for download URLs. |

## `before`
Top-level lifecycle hooks for `before` and `after` blocks. Each block carries a list of hook commands that run around the entire pipeline (not individual stages).

The canonical key is `hooks:` for both `before:` and `after:` to the conventional spelling. The `post:` spelling is accepted as a serde alias on `hooks` for back-compat with the previous anodizer spelling; users with `after: { post: [...] }` keep working and a deprecation warning is logged when both spellings appear in the same block (see `HooksConfig::merge_hook_aliases`).
| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `hooks` | list of HookEntry | тАФ | Commands to run when the block fires. The wire format accepts either `hooks:` (canonical) or the legacy `post:` spelling; both fold into this field at parse time. |
| `post` | list of HookEntry | тАФ | Legacy alias for `hooks:` (anodizer pre-v0.4). Always `None` after parsing тАФ `merge_hook_aliases` collapses it into `hooks`. Present on the struct only because `Deserialize` writes through it before the fold step. |

## `before_publish`
Top-level lifecycle hooks for `before` and `after` blocks. Each block carries a list of hook commands that run around the entire pipeline (not individual stages).

The canonical key is `hooks:` for both `before:` and `after:` to the conventional spelling. The `post:` spelling is accepted as a serde alias on `hooks` for back-compat with the previous anodizer spelling; users with `after: { post: [...] }` keep working and a deprecation warning is logged when both spellings appear in the same block (see `HooksConfig::merge_hook_aliases`).
| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `hooks` | list of HookEntry | тАФ | Commands to run when the block fires. The wire format accepts either `hooks:` (canonical) or the legacy `post:` spelling; both fold into this field at parse time. |
| `post` | list of HookEntry | тАФ | Legacy alias for `hooks:` (anodizer pre-v0.4). Always `None` after parsing тАФ `merge_hook_aliases` collapses it into `hooks`. Present on the struct only because `Deserialize` writes through it before the fold step. |

## `binary_signs`
| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `args` | list of string | тАФ | Arguments passed to the signing command (supports templates with ${artifact} and ${signature}). |
| `artifacts` | string | тАФ | Artifact types to sign: "all", "archive", "binary", "checksum", "package", "sbom" (default: "none"). |
| `authenticode` | AuthenticodeConfig | тАФ | Authenticode (Windows PE/MSI) signing backend. When set, this sign config signs Windows artifacts in place via osslsigncode (Linux/cross) or signtool (Windows) instead of producing a detached cosign/gpg signature. The signing command, argv, timestamp URL, and artifact selector are all derived; supply only the cert (a secret). |
| `certificate` | string | тАФ | Certificate file to embed in the signature (Cosign bundle signing). |
| `cmd` | string | тАФ | Signing command to invoke (default: "cosign" or "gpg"). |
| `env` | list of string | тАФ | Environment variables passed to the signing command. |
| `id` | string | тАФ | Unique identifier for this sign config. |
| `ids` | list of string | тАФ | Build IDs filter: only sign artifacts from builds whose `id` is in this list. |
| `if` | string | тАФ | Template-conditional: skip this sign config if rendered result is "false" or empty. |
| `output` | StringOrBool | тАФ | Capture and log stdout/stderr of the signing command. Accepts bool or template string (e.g., "{{ IsSnapshot }}"). |
| `signature` | string | тАФ | Signature output filename template (supports templates). |
| `stdin` | string | тАФ | Content written to the signing command's stdin. |
| `stdin_file` | string | тАФ | Path to a file whose content is written to the signing command's stdin. |
| `verify` | SignVerifyConfig | тАФ | Post-sign verification knobs. Verification is ON by default wherever its inputs are derivable (keyed cosign, keyless cosign on GitHub Actions, gpg); set `verify: { enabled: false }` to disable, or supply the keyless certificate identity / issuer when they cannot be derived from the environment. |

## `changelog`
| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `abbrev` | integer | тАФ | Hash abbreviation length. Default: 0 (no truncation, emit the full SHA). Set to -1 to omit the hash entirely; positive values truncate to N chars. Values below `-1` are clamped to `-1` (a `git log --abbrev=N` would otherwise reject `-2`, `-3`, ...). |
| `ai` | ChangelogAiConfig | тАФ | AI-powered changelog enhancement configuration. |
| `divider` | string | тАФ | Divider string inserted between changelog groups (e.g. `"---"`). Supports templates. |
| `files` | ChangelogFilesConfig | тАФ | Changelog file-layout controls: which `CHANGELOG.md` files a release writes (per-crate vs the aggregate root). Separate from the content-generation keys above (`use`, `format`, `groups`, `filters`, `paths`, `sort`, ...) so file management and content concerns stay orthogonal. See `ChangelogFilesConfig`. |
| `filters` | ChangelogFilters | тАФ | Commit message filters to include or exclude from the changelog. |
| `footer` | ContentSource | тАФ | Text appended to the changelog. Same shape as `header`. |
| `format` | string | тАФ | Template for each changelog commit line. Available variables: SHA (full hash), ShortSHA (abbreviated), Message (commit subject), AuthorName, AuthorEmail, Login (per-commit GitHub username), Logins (per-entry comma-separated list of GitHub usernames for that commit), AllLogins (comma-separated list of all GitHub usernames across the entire release), AuthorUsername (renders `@login` when the login is known, the plain author name otherwise).<br><br>Logins come from the SCM API backends (`use: github`/`gitea`) and тАФ when the release targets GitHub and a token is available тАФ from GitHub-API enrichment of the default `git` backend, so `use: git` changelogs render `@login` mentions too. Release bodies carry bare `@login` (GitHub autolinks them); on-disk `CHANGELOG.md` files get explicit `[@login](https://github.com/login)` links. Without a token (or offline, or with a non-GitHub remote) rendering keeps the plain author name.<br><br>Default depends on backend (the full SHA is used):<br>&bull; `git` backend (default): `"{{ SHA }} {{ Message }}"`<br>&bull; `github`/`gitlab`/`gitea` backend: `"{{ SHA }}: {{ Message }} (@Login or AuthorName <AuthorEmail>)"` тАФ falls back to `AuthorName <AuthorEmail>` when `Login` is empty.<br><br>When `abbrev < 0`, the default reduces to `"{{ Message }}"` (no hash prefix). |
| `groups` | list of ChangelogGroup | тАФ | Groups for organizing changelog entries by commit message prefix. |
| `header` | ContentSource | тАФ | Text prepended to the changelog. Inline string, `from_file: <path>`, or `from_url: <url>` тАФ symmetric with the release block's header/footer so users can compose headers from a templated file or remote endpoint (the upstream uses a plain string here; anodizer extends to ContentSource for consistency with `release.header`). |
| `paths` | list of string | тАФ | Optional path filter that NARROWS the per-crate scope by intersection тАФ it never replaces it. Each changelog track already scopes to its own commits (a per-crate track to its crate directory; the aggregate to the union of every crate directory plus the workspace manifests). When set, `paths` further restricts that derived scope to commits whose touched files match these globs; it can only ever drop commits, never widen to another track's directory. A `paths` value that is a superset of the derived scope (e.g. `["crates/**", "Cargo.toml", "Cargo.lock"]` over a workspace) is therefore a no-op тАФ and so is the recommended default of leaving `paths` unset, where scoping is fully derived. The same derived scope and intersect drive all three changelog formats (`keep-a-changelog`, `json`, and `release-notes`), so they cannot drift.<br><br>With `use: git` the intersect is precise (commits are filtered by their touched files). With `use: github` only the first path is used for API queries; with `use: gitlab` / `gitea` path filtering is unsupported, so a narrowing `paths` there is coarse and a warning is emitted. Supports template rendering. |
| `skip` | StringOrBool | тАФ | Skip changelog generation. Accepts bool or template string (e.g. `"{{ if IsSnapshot }}true{{ endif }}"` for conditional skip).<br><br>Accepts `disable:` as an alias so imported configs (which use `changelog.disable:`) parse cleanly without a rename. Anodizer's broader convention is `skip:` (mirrors `release.skip_upload`, stage-level `skip:` flags), so the canonical key stays `skip:`. |
| `snapshot` | bool | тАФ | When `true`, render the changelog even in snapshot mode. Anodizer matches the default (skip changelog on snapshot) and lets users opt back in here for local preview / draft generation. Wired in `crates/stage-changelog/src/lib.rs::ChangelogStage::run`. |
| `sort` | string | тАФ | Sort order for changelog entries: "asc" or "desc" (default: "asc"). |
| `title` | string | тАФ | Title heading for the changelog. Default: "Changelog". Supports templates. |
| `use` | string | тАФ | Changelog source: `"git"` (default), `"github"`, or `"github-native"`. `"github"` fetches commits via the GitHub API, enriching entries with author login information (available as the `{{ Logins }}` per-entry template variable and the `{{ AllLogins }}` release-wide variable). `"github-native"` delegates entirely to GitHub's auto-generated notes. |

## `cloudsmiths`
CloudSmith publisher configuration. Pushes packages to CloudSmith repositories.
| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `component` | string | тАФ | Debian component name (e.g. "main"). |
| `distributions` | map | тАФ | Distribution mapping per format. Each entry accepts either a single slug (`deb: "ubuntu/focal"`) or an array of slugs (`deb: ["ubuntu/focal", "ubuntu/jammy"]`); the array form issues one upload per entry. |
| `exclude` | list of string | тАФ | Glob patterns matched against each artifact's file name; anodizer drops any artifact whose name matches at least one glob from THIS CloudSmith target only. Use it to keep heavy sidecars off a given repository while packages still upload. Composes with `ids:` and `formats:` (all filters apply). `None`/empty keeps everything.<br><br>```yaml cloudsmiths: - organization: my-org repository: my-repo exclude: ["*.sha256", "*.sig", "*.cdx.json"] ``` |
| `formats` | list of string | тАФ | Package format filter: only publish artifacts matching these formats. |
| `ids` | list of string | тАФ | Build IDs filter: only publish artifacts from builds whose `id` is in this list. |
| `if` | string | тАФ | Template-conditional gate: when the rendered result is falsy (`"false"` / `"0"` / `"no"` / empty), the CloudSmith publisher is skipped. Render failure hard-errors. Config key: `cloudsmiths[].if:`. |
| `keep_versions` | integer | тАФ | Retain only the `N` most-recent release versions of each published package, pruning older ones from the CloudSmith repository after a successful upload.<br><br>This is **opt-in** and **destructive**: leaving it unset (the default) prunes nothing. When set, after the just-uploaded artifacts are confirmed present the publisher lists every version of *this* package in the repository, ranks the distinct release versions by SemVer (newest first), keeps the top `N` тАФ which always includes the version just published тАФ and issues `DELETE` for every artifact (all formats and architectures) belonging to versions ranked beyond `N`. Other packages sharing the repository are never touched.<br><br>All package formats of one release are treated as the same version: the deb/rpm epoch (`1:0.9.1-1`) and apk revision (`0.9.1-r1`) suffixes are normalized to the base SemVer (`0.9.1`) before ranking, so keeping `2` versions keeps every `.deb`/`.rpm`/`.apk` of the two newest releases.<br><br>Pruning is **best-effort**: it runs only after the upload (the real work) has already succeeded, is skipped entirely in dry-run and snapshot mode, and a list/delete failure emits a prominent warning and continues rather than failing the release or rolling anything back. `keep_versions: 0` is rejected тАФ anodizer never prunes every version.<br><br>Primarily a remedy for storage-capped repositories (e.g. the CloudSmith free plan's 500 MB limit, which offers no server-side retention policy).<br><br>```yaml cloudsmiths: - organization: acme repository: tools keep_versions: 3   # keep the 3 newest releases, prune older ones ``` |
| `organization` | string | тАФ | CloudSmith organization slug. |
| `repository` | string | тАФ | CloudSmith repository slug. |
| `republish` | StringOrBool | тАФ | When true, allow republishing over existing package versions. |
| `required` | bool | тАФ | Override whether this publisher failing should fail the overall release.<br><br>Default: `false` тАФ a failure here is logged but does not abort the release. Set to `true` to fail the release on any error. |
| `retain_on_rollback` | bool | тАФ | When `true`, a triggered rollback leaves this publisher's work in place rather than attempting to undo it. Default `false`. |
| `secret_name` | string | тАФ | Environment variable name containing the CloudSmith API key. |
| `skip` | StringOrBool | тАФ | Template-conditional skip: if rendered result is `"true"`, skip this publisher. |

## `crates`
| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `after` | HooksConfig | тАФ | Hooks that run inside THIS crate's scope at the end of the release, after the crate's publish dispatch (and post-publish verification) completes. Per-crate counterpart of the top-level `after:` (which fires once around the whole release). Same per-crate firing semantics across all modes, template surface, and abort semantics as the per-crate `before:`. |
| `app_bundles` | list of AppBundleConfig | тАФ | macOS app bundle configurations for this crate. |
| `archives` | list of ArchiveConfig | `[]` | Archive configurations for this crate. Set to false to disable archiving, or provide an array of archive configs. |
| `before` | HooksConfig | тАФ | Hooks that run inside THIS crate's scope at the start of the release, before the build. Distinct from the top-level `before:`, which fires ONCE around the whole release; these fire once per crate with that crate's version/tag template vars anchored, so `cmd` / `dir` / `env` / `if` render against the crate's own `Version` / `Tag` / `ProjectName`. A non-zero exit aborts the release.<br><br>Fires once per crate in EVERY multi-crate mode тАФ workspace per-crate AND workspace lockstep with multiple publisher crates тАФ in both a full `anodizer release` and `anodizer release --publish-only`, matching the per-crate iteration of `before_publish:` and the publishers. With an explicit `--crate` subset only the selected crates' hooks fire. No-op in a single-crate config with no `crates:` block (use the top-level `before:` there). |
| `before_publish` | HooksConfig | тАФ | Hooks that run immediately before THIS crate's publishers dispatch, once per matching artifact (the same per-artifact semantics as the top-level `before_publish:`), scoped to the crate's own artifacts and template vars. Honors the per-entry `ids:` / `artifacts:` filters. A non-zero exit aborts the release before that crate publishes to any registry. The top-level `before_publish:` still fires once over the full artifact set; this one targets a single crate's artifacts. |
| `binstall` | BinstallConfig | тАФ | cargo-binstall metadata configuration for this crate. |
| `blobs` | list of BlobConfig | тАФ | Cloud storage (S3/GCS/Azure) upload configurations for this crate. |
| `builds` | list of BuildConfig | тАФ | Build configurations for this crate. One entry per binary by default. |
| `checksum` | ChecksumConfig | тАФ | Checksum configuration for this crate. |
| `cross` | CrossStrategy | тАФ | Cross-compilation strategy for this crate: auto, zigbuild, cross, or cargo. |
| `depends_on` | list of string | тАФ | Other crates this crate depends on; ensures release ordering. |
| `dmgs` | list of DmgConfig | тАФ | macOS DMG disk image configurations for this crate. |
| `docker_digest` | DockerDigestConfig | тАФ | Docker image digest file configuration for this crate. |
| `docker_manifests` | list of DockerManifestConfig | тАФ | Docker multi-platform manifest configurations for this crate. |
| `dockers_v2` | list of DockerV2Config | тАФ | Docker V2 image build configurations for this crate (canonical API: images+tags, annotations, build_args, sbom, disable). The legacy `docker:` block was removed; this is the only docker surface. The `docker_v2:` spelling is still accepted via serde alias for back-compat. |
| `flatpaks` | list of FlatpakConfig | тАФ | Linux Flatpak bundle configurations for this crate. |
| `msis` | list of MsiConfig | тАФ | Windows MSI installer configurations for this crate. |
| `name` | string | тАФ | Crate name as published (must match the Cargo.toml package name). |
| `nfpms` | list of NfpmConfig | тАФ | Linux package (deb, rpm, apk) configurations for this crate. Renamed from `nfpm:` (singular) for spelling parity with `Defaults.nfpms` and the rest of the plural-name per-crate packaging lists (`dmgs`, `msis`, `pkgs`, `nsis`, ...). The `nfpm:` spelling is still accepted via serde alias for back-compat. |
| `no_unique_dist_dir` | StringOrBool | тАФ | When true (or template evaluating to "true"), all build outputs are placed in a flat `dist/` directory instead of `dist/{target}/`. |
| `nsis` | list of NsisConfig | тАФ | NSIS installer configurations for this crate. |
| `path` | string | тАФ | Relative path to the crate directory from the project root. |
| `pkgs` | list of PkgConfig | тАФ | macOS PKG installer configurations for this crate. |
| `publish` | PublishConfig | тАФ | Publishing targets (Homebrew, Scoop, AUR, etc.) for this crate. |
| `release` | ReleaseConfig | тАФ | GitHub release configuration for this crate. |
| `snapcrafts` | list of SnapcraftConfig | тАФ | Snapcraft package configurations for this crate. |
| `tag_template` | string | тАФ | Git tag template used to tag and identify releases (supports templates). Overrides `defaults.crates.tag_template`. When both are unset, resolves to `CrateConfig::DEFAULT_TAG_TEMPLATE` тАФ use `resolved_tag_template()` rather than reading this field directly. |
| `universal_binaries` | list of UniversalBinaryConfig | тАФ | macOS universal binary (fat binary) configurations for this crate. |
| `version` | string | тАФ | Pinned semver version. When set, `anodizer bump --strict` refuses to edit this crate's `Cargo.toml` to anything other than this value; without `--strict`, the bump proceeds with a warning. Lets a release captain freeze a crate's version while still running broad `--workspace` bumps. |
| `version_files` | list of string | тАФ | Repo-committed files that embed this crate's release version outside `Cargo.toml` (repo-root-relative path strings). At `tag` time each file has its occurrences of the old version rewritten to the new version тАФ both bare and `v`-prefixed forms, word-boundary anchored тАФ and is staged into the same bump commit as this crate's `Cargo.toml`. Overrides the workspace-level `defaults.version_files`. |
| `version_sync` | VersionSyncConfig | тАФ | Automatic version number synchronization configuration for this crate. |

## `defaults`
Workspace-level defaults that path-mirror the `CrateConfig` (and select top-level `Config`) shape. Each field here is folded into every resolved crate by `defaults_merge::apply_defaults` according to the deep-merge / merge-by-identity semantics documented in `defaults_merge`.

Multi-publisher fields are single-struct on both sides today: defaults supplies one struct per publisher, and per-crate `publish.*` fields are also single-struct. A future change may introduce list-or-scalar via `OneOrMany<T>` on the per-crate side so a crate can declare multiple homebrew taps / scoop buckets / etc.; the defaults side would stay single-struct and merge into the first per-crate entry by identity.
| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `app_bundles` | AppBundleConfig | тАФ | Default app-bundle settings applied to all crates. |
| `archives` | ArchiveConfig | тАФ | Default archive settings applied to all crates. |
| `binary_signs` | SignConfig | тАФ | Default binary-signing settings. |
| `builds` | BuildConfig | тАФ | Default build settings applied to every crate's builds (deep-merged into each `CrateConfig.builds[]` entry by identity on `id`/`binary`). |
| `checksum` | ChecksumConfig | тАФ | Default checksum settings applied to all crates. Mirrors `CrateConfig.checksum` so checksum config can be hoisted to defaults. |
| `crates` | DefaultsCrateBlock | тАФ | Crate-axis defaults marker. Only valid when top-level `crates:` is set. Reserved for per-crate overrides keyed by crate id (future waves). |
| `cross` | CrossStrategy | тАФ | Default cross-compilation strategy: auto, zigbuild, cross, or cargo. Mirrors `CrateConfig.cross` so the strategy can be hoisted to defaults. |
| `dmgs` | DmgConfig | тАФ | Default DMG settings applied to all crates. |
| `docker_signs` | DockerSignConfig | тАФ | Default Docker image signing settings. |
| `dockers_v2` | DockerV2Config | тАФ | Default Docker (V2 API) image settings applied to all crates. The `docker_v2:` spelling is still accepted via serde alias for back-compat. |
| `env` | list of string | тАФ | Default environment variables (`KEY=VALUE` strings) hoisted across crates. |
| `flatpaks` | FlatpakConfig | тАФ | Default flatpak settings applied to all crates. |
| `install_scripts` | InstallScriptConfig | тАФ | Default install-script settings applied to all crates. |
| `makeselves` | MakeselfConfig | тАФ | Default makeself settings applied to all crates. |
| `msis` | MsiConfig | тАФ | Default MSI settings applied to all crates. |
| `nfpms` | NfpmConfig | тАФ | Default nfpm (deb/rpm/apk) settings applied to all crates. |
| `notarize` | NotarizeConfig | тАФ | Default macOS notarization settings. |
| `nsis` | NsisConfig | тАФ | Default NSIS settings applied to all crates. |
| `pkgs` | PkgConfig | тАФ | Default macOS PKG settings applied to all crates. |
| `publish` | PublishDefaults | тАФ | Default publisher configurations (single-struct per publisher). Per-crate `publish.*` entries are merged into these by identity. |
| `sbom` | SbomConfig | тАФ | Default SBOM generation settings. |
| `sign` | SignConfig | тАФ | Default artifact signing settings. |
| `snapcrafts` | SnapcraftConfig | тАФ | Default snapcraft settings applied to all crates. |
| `source` | SourceConfig | тАФ | Default source-archive settings applied to all crates. |
| `srpms` | SrpmConfig | тАФ | Default SRPM settings applied to all crates. |
| `targets` | list of string | тАФ | Default build targets (e.g., ["x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu", "aarch64-apple-darwin"]). |
| `upx` | UpxConfig | тАФ | Default UPX compression settings applied to all crates. |
| `version_files` | list of string | тАФ | Default repo-committed files whose embedded release version is rewritten at `tag` time (repo-root-relative path strings). Hoisted across crates; folded into each crate's `version_files` by `defaults_merge` when the crate does not set its own list. Mirrors `CrateConfig.version_files`. |
| `workspaces` | DefaultsWorkspaceBlock | тАФ | Workspace-axis defaults marker. Only valid when top-level `workspaces:` is set. Reserved for per-workspace overrides keyed by workspace name (future waves). |

## `docker_signs`
| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `args` | list of string | тАФ | Arguments passed to the signing command (supports templates). |
| `artifacts` | string | тАФ | Docker artifact types to sign: "all", "image", or "manifest" (default: "none"). |
| `certificate` | string | тАФ | Certificate file to embed in the signature (Cosign bundle signing). |
| `cmd` | string | тАФ | Signing command to invoke (default: "cosign"). |
| `env` | list of string | тАФ | Environment variables passed to the signing command. |
| `id` | string | тАФ | Unique identifier for this docker sign config. |
| `ids` | list of string | тАФ | Docker config IDs filter: only sign images from configs whose `id` is in this list. |
| `if` | string | тАФ | Template-conditional: skip this docker sign config if rendered result is "false" or empty. |
| `output` | StringOrBool | тАФ | Capture and log stdout/stderr of the docker signing command. |
| `signature` | string | тАФ | Signature output filename template (supports templates). |
| `stdin` | string | тАФ | Content written to the signing command's stdin. |
| `stdin_file` | string | тАФ | Path to a file whose content is written to the signing command's stdin. |
| `verify` | SignVerifyConfig | тАФ | Post-sign verification knobs тАФ see `SignVerifyConfig`. Docker signatures are verified with `cosign verify` against the registry the sign just pushed to. |

## `dockerhub`
DockerHub description sync configuration. Pushes image descriptions and README content to DockerHub repositories.
| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `description` | string | тАФ | Short description for the DockerHub repository (max 100 chars). |
| `full_description` | DockerHubFullDescription | тАФ | Full description (README) source for the DockerHub repository. |
| `if` | string | тАФ | Template-conditional gate: when the rendered result is falsy (`"false"` / `"0"` / `"no"` / empty), the DockerHub publisher is skipped. Render failure hard-errors. Exposes the `dockerhub[].if:` conditional gate; distinct from `skip:` (which expresses "always skip") and provides config-import parity. |
| `images` | list of string | тАФ | DockerHub image names to update (e.g. `myorg/myapp`). |
| `required` | bool | тАФ | Override whether this publisher failing should fail the overall release.<br><br>Default: `false` тАФ a failure here is logged but does not abort the release. Set to `true` to fail the release on any error. |
| `retain_on_rollback` | bool | тАФ | When `true`, a triggered rollback leaves this publisher's work in place rather than attempting to undo it. Default `false`. |
| `secret_name` | string | тАФ | Environment variable name containing the DockerHub token. |
| `skip` | StringOrBool | тАФ | Skip this publisher. Accepts bool or template string. Accepts the legacy `disable:` spelling via serde alias for back-compat with imported configs (the legacy `disable:` spelling). |
| `username` | string | тАФ | DockerHub username for authentication. |

## `gemfury`
GemFury package registry publisher configuration.

Pushes deb / rpm / apk artifacts to `https://push.fury.io/<account>`. Authenticates via HTTP Basic auth using the push token as the username (empty password) тАФ the conventional Fury push surface.
| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `account` | string | тАФ | GemFury account name. Required; rendered through the template engine so `account: "{{ Env.MY_FURY_ACCOUNT }}"` works. |
| `api_secret_name` | string | тАФ | Environment variable name carrying the API (delete) token. Default `FURY_API_TOKEN`. |
| `api_token` | string | тАФ | Optional API token used by rollback to issue `DELETE /<account>/packages/<name>/versions/<version>`. When unset, the env var named by `api_secret_name` (default `FURY_API_TOKEN`) is consulted at rollback time. If both are absent at rollback time, the publisher falls back to a manual-cleanup warn. |
| `exclude` | list of string | тАФ | Glob patterns matched against each artifact's file name; anodizer drops any artifact whose name matches at least one glob from THIS GemFury target only. Use it to keep heavy sidecars off the account while packages still upload. Composes with `ids:` and the format filter (all filters apply). `None`/empty keeps everything.<br><br>```yaml gemfury: - account: my-account exclude: ["*.sha256", "*.sig", "*.cdx.json"] ``` |
| `formats` | list of string | тАФ | Package format filter: only push artifacts matching these formats. Defaults to `["apk", "deb", "rpm"]`. |
| `id` | string | тАФ | Unique identifier for selecting this entry from the CLI (`--id=...`). |
| `ids` | list of string | тАФ | Build IDs filter: only include artifacts whose archive `id` is in this list. |
| `if` | string | тАФ | Template-conditional gate: when the rendered result is falsy (`"false"` / `"0"` / `"no"` / empty), the GemFury publisher entry is skipped. Render failure hard-errors. Exposes the `gemfury[].if:` conditional gate; distinct from `skip:` (which expresses "always skip") and provides config-import parity. |
| `required` | bool | тАФ | Override whether this publisher failing should fail the overall release.<br><br>Default: `true` тАФ GemFury is a Manager-group publisher (mutable but reversible via the delete API), so a failed publish aborts by default to avoid surprising the operator with a half-released version. Set to `false` to log failures but continue. |
| `retain_on_rollback` | bool | тАФ | When `true`, a triggered rollback leaves this publisher's work in place rather than attempting to undo it. Default `false`. |
| `secret_name` | string | тАФ | Environment variable name carrying the push token. Default `FURY_PUSH_TOKEN`. The actual token VALUE is read from this env var at publish/rollback time. |
| `skip` | StringOrBool | тАФ | Template-conditional skip: if rendered result is `"true"`, skip this publisher entry. Accepts bool or template string. Accepts the legacy `disable:` spelling via serde alias for back-compat with imported `gemfury[].disable:` configs. |
| `token` | string | тАФ | Push token used as the HTTP Basic auth username (empty password). When unset, the env var named by `secret_name` (default `FURY_PUSH_TOKEN`) is consulted at publish time. NEVER logged. |

## `git`
Git-level tag discovery and sorting settings.

Controls how anodizer discovers and orders tags when determining the current and previous versions. This is separate from `TagConfig`, which controls version *bumping* logic.
| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `ignore_tag_prefixes` | list of string | тАФ | Tag prefixes to ignore during version detection (supports templates). Tags starting with any prefix in this list are excluded. The ignore-tag-prefixes feature. |
| `ignore_tags` | list of string | тАФ | Tag patterns to ignore during version detection (supports templates). Tags matching any pattern in this list are excluded from version detection entirely. |
| `prerelease_suffix` | string | тАФ | Suffix that identifies pre-release tags for sorting purposes. When set, tags ending with this suffix are treated as pre-releases and sorted accordingly during tag discovery. |
| `tag_sort` | string | тАФ | How to sort git tags when determining the latest version.<br><br>Accepted values: - `"-version:refname"` (default) тАФ lexicographic version sort on the tag name. - `"-version:creatordate"` тАФ sort by the tag's creation date (newest first). - `"semver"` тАФ strict SemVer 2.0.0 ordering computed in Rust; prereleases sort below their release per spec section 11. Bypasses git's native sort. - `"smartsemver"` тАФ same ordering as `"semver"`, but when the current version (resolved from the template `Version` variable) is non-prerelease, prerelease tags are filtered out before previous-tag selection. Prevents `v0.2.0-beta.3` from being picked as the predecessor of `v0.2.0` (which would otherwise produce an empty changelog). |

## `gitea_urls`
Custom Gitea API/download URLs for self-hosted Gitea installations. Gitea API/download URL overrides.
| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `api` | string | тАФ | Gitea API base URL (e.g. `https://gitea.example.com/api/v1/`). |
| `download` | string | тАФ | Gitea download URL for release assets. |
| `skip_tls_verify` | bool | тАФ | When true, skip TLS certificate verification for the custom URLs. |

## `github_urls`
Custom GitHub API/upload/download URLs for GitHub Enterprise installations. GitHub API/download URL overrides.
| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `api` | string | тАФ | GitHub API base URL (e.g. `https://github.example.com/api/v3/`). |
| `download` | string | тАФ | GitHub download URL for release assets (e.g. `https://github.example.com/`). |
| `skip_tls_verify` | bool | тАФ | When true, skip TLS certificate verification for the custom URLs. |
| `upload` | string | тАФ | GitHub upload URL for release assets (e.g. `https://github.example.com/api/uploads/`). |

## `gitlab_urls`
Custom GitLab API/download URLs for self-hosted GitLab installations. GitLab API/download URL overrides.
| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `api` | string | тАФ | GitLab API base URL (e.g. `https://gitlab.example.com/api/v4/`). |
| `download` | string | тАФ | GitLab download URL for release assets. |
| `skip_tls_verify` | bool | тАФ | When true, skip TLS certificate verification for the custom URLs. |
| `use_job_token` | bool | тАФ | When true, use the CI_JOB_TOKEN for authentication instead of a personal token. |
| `use_package_registry` | bool | тАФ | When true, use the GitLab Package Registry for uploads instead of Generic Packages. |

## `homebrew_casks`
Unified Homebrew Cask configuration.

Used at both call-sites: - `homebrew_casks:` тАФ top-level array; carries `repository`, `commit_author`, `directory`, `ids`, `url`, structured `uninstall`/`zap`, etc. - `crates[].publish.homebrew_cask:` тАФ per-crate override; same shape, with `url_template` as the simpler URL alternative.

Fields from both original types are present; any field may be `None` at either call-site. The union avoids a two-type bifurcation while keeping both axes.
| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `alternative_names` | list of string | тАФ | Alternative cask names (aliases). |
| `app` | string | тАФ | macOS .app bundle name (e.g. "MyApp.app"). |
| `binaries` | list of HomebrewCaskBinary | тАФ | Binary stubs to create in /usr/local/bin.<br><br>Each entry is either a bare string (`"my-cli"` тЖТ emits `binary "my-cli"`) or a structured `{ name, target }` object (`{ name: "my-cli", target: "mycli" }` тЖТ emits `binary "my-cli", target: "mycli"`). The `target:` form mirrors the Homebrew Ruby cask DSL for binary renames тАФ without it, a wrapped binary installs at the wrong path. Cask binary entry. |
| `binary` | string | тАФ | Deprecated singular spelling of `binaries`. The upstream replaced `binary: foo` with `binaries: [foo]`; this field captures the legacy spelling so imported configs keep parsing. `apply_homebrew_cask_legacy_singulars` folds the value into `binaries` at config-load time and emits a one-time deprecation warning per occurrence. The field is excluded from serialization so a round-tripped config emits only the canonical plural form. |
| `caveats` | string | тАФ | Custom caveats shown after install. |
| `commit_author` | CommitAuthorConfig | тАФ | Commit author with optional signing. |
| `commit_msg_template` | string | тАФ | Custom commit message template. Default: "Brew cask update for {{ ProjectName }} version {{ Tag }}" |
| `completions` | HomebrewCaskCompletions | тАФ | Shell completion definitions. |
| `conflicts` | list of HomebrewCaskConflictEntry | тАФ | Conflicting casks or formulae. |
| `custom_block` | string | тАФ | Arbitrary Ruby code inserted into the cask block. |
| `dependencies` | list of HomebrewCaskDependencyEntry | тАФ | Cask dependencies (other casks or formulae). |
| `description` | string | тАФ | Cask description. |
| `directory` | string | тАФ | Subdirectory in the tap repo for cask placement (default: "Casks"). |
| `generate_completions_from_executable` | HomebrewCaskGeneratedCompletions | тАФ | Auto-generate shell completions from an executable. |
| `homepage` | string | тАФ | Project homepage URL. |
| `hooks` | HomebrewCaskHooks | тАФ | Pre/post install/uninstall hooks. |
| `ids` | list of string | тАФ | Build IDs filter: only include artifacts from builds whose `id` is in this list. |
| `if` | string | тАФ | Template-conditional gate: when the rendered result is falsy (`"false"` / `"0"` / `"no"` / empty), the Homebrew Cask config is skipped. Render failure hard-errors. Config key: `homebrew_casks[].if:`. |
| `license` | string | тАФ | License identifier (SPDX). |
| `livecheck` | HomebrewLivecheck | тАФ | `livecheck` stanza configuration for the cask. When unset, the cask emits `livecheck do\n  skip "Auto-generated on release."\nend` (a binary cask's download URL/sha256 are rewritten on every release, so `brew livecheck` has nothing stable to poll). Set `strategy:` / `url:` / `regex:` (with `skip: false`) to opt into active version detection тАФ the same shape a Homebrew cask `livecheck do тАж end` block accepts. Reuses the formula `livecheck` config type. |
| `manpage` | string | тАФ | Deprecated singular spelling of `manpages`. The upstream replaced `manpage: foo.1` with `manpages: [foo.1]`; this field captures the legacy spelling so imported configs keep parsing. `apply_homebrew_cask_legacy_singulars` folds the value into `manpages` at config-load time and emits a one-time deprecation warning per occurrence. The field is excluded from serialization so a round-tripped config emits only the canonical plural form. |
| `manpages` | list of string | тАФ | Manual page references to install. |
| `name` | string | тАФ | Cask name (default: crate / project name). |
| `repository` | RepositoryConfig | тАФ | Unified repository config for the Homebrew tap. |
| `required` | bool | тАФ | Override whether this publisher failing should fail the overall release.<br><br>Default: `false` тАФ a failure here is logged but does not abort the release. Set to `true` to fail the release on any error. |
| `retain_on_rollback` | bool | тАФ | When `true`, a triggered rollback leaves this publisher's work in place rather than attempting to undo it. Default `false`. |
| `service` | string | тАФ | Homebrew service definition. |
| `skip_upload` | StringOrBool | тАФ | Skip publishing the cask. `"true"` always skips; `"auto"` skips for prerelease versions. Accepts bool or template string. |
| `uninstall` | HomebrewCaskUninstall | тАФ | Structured uninstall stanza configuration. |
| `update_existing_pr` | StringOrBool | тАФ | When true, force-push the updated cask file to the existing PR branch when a PR for the same head branch already exists. The PR content is updated in place rather than creating a duplicate. When false (default), the push is skipped and a warning is emitted so the operator sees that the publisher did not update the PR. |
| `url` | HomebrewCaskURL | тАФ | Structured download URL configuration (top-level axis). |
| `url_template` | string | тАФ | Simple URL template for the .dmg/.zip download (per-crate shorthand).<br><br>Cannot be combined with `url.template:` тАФ set one or the other. If both are present, config validation rejects the config at parse time. Use `url:` for the structured form (verified domain, custom headers, etc.) or `url_template:` for a bare string shorthand тАФ never both simultaneously. |
| `zap` | HomebrewCaskUninstall | тАФ | Deep uninstall (zap) stanza configuration. |

## `homebrew_cores`
homebrew-core formula-bump publisher configuration.

Bumps an EXISTING formula in `Homebrew/homebrew-core` (or any formula repository override) purely through the GitHub API тАФ no clone, no `brew` invocation. The formula file's `url` (or `tag:`/`revision:` pair), `sha256`, and `version` stanzas are rewritten to the new release, the change is committed to a branch, and a pull request is opened against the formula repository. Each `homebrew_cores[]` entry bumps one formula.

Every field is optional: the formula name defaults to the crate name, the target repository defaults to `Homebrew/homebrew-core`, the formula path defaults to the sharded core layout (`Formula/<letter>/<name>.rb`, falling back to the flat `Formula/<name>.rb`), and the download URL defaults to the GitHub source tarball for the release tag.

```yaml homebrew_cores: - name: my-tool ```
| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `commit_author` | CommitAuthorConfig | тАФ | Commit author for the formula-bump commit, with optional signing. Its `use_github_app_token` is the canonical homebrew-core knob: when set, the `author`/`committer` fields are omitted from the contents-API commit so GitHub attributes it to the token's own account тАФ the `<app-slug>[bot]` identity a GitHub App workflow needs to satisfy homebrew-core's DCO/CLA policy. Otherwise the resolved `name`/`email` (config тЖТ local git identity тЖТ the anodizer default) author the commit.<br><br>Note: `signing` has no effect here тАФ formula bumps commit through the GitHub contents API, which GitHub signs server-side; the git `-c commit.gpgsign` path the tap/winget/krew publishers use does not apply.<br><br>```yaml homebrew_cores: - commit_author: use_github_app_token: true ``` |
| `commit_msg_template` | string | тАФ | Templated commit message (also the PR title). Default: `"<formula> <version>"` тАФ the message form homebrew-core's CI expects for version bumps. |
| `direct_commit` | StringOrBool | тАФ | Commit straight to the base branch instead of opening a pull request. Accepts bool or template string. Only honored for formula repositories you can push to тАФ bumps targeting `Homebrew/homebrew-core` always go through a fork + PR, because homebrew-core never accepts direct pushes.<br><br>A back-compat alias for `repository.pull_request.enabled: false`, the preferred spelling shared with the tap/scoop/nix publishers тАФ either one selects the direct-commit path (both are still overridden by the always-fork-and-PR rule for `Homebrew/homebrew-core`). |
| `download_url` | string | тАФ | Templated download URL written into the formula's `url` stanza. Defaults to the GitHub source tarball for the release tag: `https://github.com/<owner>/<repo>/archive/refs/tags/<tag>.tar.gz` (owner/repo derived from the crate's release repository, then the git remote). |
| `id` | string | тАФ | Unique identifier for selecting this entry from the CLI (`--id=...`). |
| `ids` | list of string | тАФ | Crate scoping: when set, the formula `name` default is derived from the first crate named here instead of the workspace's primary crate. The workspace per-crate pattern тАФ one `homebrew_cores[]` entry per crate, each scoped by `ids:`. |
| `if` | string | тАФ | Template-conditional gate: when the rendered result is falsy (`"false"` / `"0"` / `"no"` / empty), this entry is skipped. Render failure hard-errors. |
| `name` | string | тАФ | Formula name (templated). Defaults to the scoped crate name (see `ids:`), then the workspace's primary crate name, then the project name. |
| `path` | string | тАФ | Formula file path inside the repository (templated). Defaults to the homebrew-core sharded layout `Formula/<first-letter>/<name>.rb`, falling back to the flat `Formula/<name>.rb` used by most personal taps when the sharded path does not exist. |
| `repository` | RepositoryConfig | тАФ | Target formula repository. Defaults to `Homebrew/homebrew-core`. Carries the auth token override (`repository.token`), the base branch (`repository.branch`, default: the repo's default branch), and PR settings (`repository.pull_request.draft` / `.body`).<br><br>```yaml homebrew_cores: - repository: { owner: my-org, name: my-formulas } ``` |
| `required` | bool | тАФ | Override whether this publisher failing should fail the overall release.<br><br>Default: `false` тАФ the bump is a PR that can be re-opened by hand, so a failure here is logged but does not abort the release. Set to `true` to fail the release on any error. |
| `retain_on_rollback` | bool | тАФ | When `true`, a triggered rollback leaves the opened pull request in place rather than closing it. Default `false`. |
| `sha256` | string | тАФ | Hex SHA-256 of the new download (templated). When unset, anodizer downloads `download_url` and hashes it тАФ the same behavior as `brew bump-formula-pr` without `--sha256`. |
| `skip` | StringOrBool | тАФ | Skip this publisher. Accepts bool or template string. Accepts the legacy `disable:` spelling via serde alias for back-compat. |
| `update_existing_pr` | StringOrBool | тАФ | When truthy, refresh the existing bump PR in place instead of skipping it: a same-version re-cut force-resets the bump branch to the current base and re-commits the rewritten formula, so the open PR carries this run's content rather than a stale earlier attempt (and no duplicate PR is opened). When falsy (default), an already-open bump PR is left untouched and a warning names this toggle. Accepts bool or template string. Mirrors `winget` / `krew` / `homebrew_cask`'s `update_existing_pr`. |

## `install_scripts`
`curl | sh` installer-script configuration.

Drives the install-script stage, which emits a deterministic POSIX `install.sh` as a release asset. At run time the script detects the host OS + architecture, maps it to the matching release archive, downloads and sha256-verifies it, extracts the binary, and installs it into an install directory (falling back to `$HOME/.local/bin` when the primary directory is not writable and no `sudo` is available).

Every field is optional: the repository slug is derived from the git `origin` remote, the installed binary names from the project name, the per-platform asset names from the release's configured targets (via the same engine SSOT that keeps cargo-binstall `pkg_url` from 404ing), and the checksums filename and tag prefix from the flagship crate that builds the project binary тАФ so a bare `install_scripts: {}` produces a fully working installer with no required input.
| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `base_url` | string | тАФ | Base URL the script downloads releases from and queries the REST API against (default: `https://github.com`). Point this at a GitHub Enterprise host to install from a self-hosted GitHub; the script derives the API base as `<base_url>/api/v3` for any non-`github.com` host. |
| `binaries` | list of string | тАФ | Binary names to install out of the extracted archive (default: a single-element list of the project name). Every name is installed, so an archive shipping multiple binaries lands them all. |
| `description` | string | тАФ | One-line description rendered into the script's header banner. |
| `filename` | string | тАФ | Output filename (default: `install.sh`). |
| `homepage` | string | тАФ | Project homepage URL rendered into the script's header banner. |
| `id` | string | тАФ | Unique identifier for this install-script config (default: "default"). |
| `install_dir` | string | тАФ | Directory the binary is installed into (default: `/usr/local/bin`). The generated script falls back to `$HOME/.local/bin` when this directory is not writable and no `sudo` is available. |
| `name` | string | тАФ | Human-readable name rendered into the script's header banner (default: the project name). |
| `repo` | string | тАФ | GitHub `owner/name` slug the script downloads releases from (default: derived from the git `origin` remote). |
| `skip` | StringOrBool | тАФ | Skip this config. Accepts bool or template string. Accepts the legacy `disable:` spelling via serde alias for back-compat with imported configs. |
| `verify_checksum` | bool | тАФ | Whether the script verifies each download's sha256 checksum before installing (default: `true`). Set `false` only for repos that publish no checksums file or `.sha256` sidecars. |

## `makeselfs`
| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `arch` | list of string | тАФ | Target architecture filter. |
| `compression` | string | тАФ | Compression algorithm: gzip, bzip2, xz, lzo, compress, or none. |
| `description` | string | тАФ | Description for LSM metadata. |
| `extra_args` | list of string | тАФ | Extra arguments passed to the makeself command. |
| `filename` | string | тАФ | Output filename template (default includes project, version, os, arch). |
| `files` | list of MakeselfFile | тАФ | Additional files to include in the archive. |
| `homepage` | string | тАФ | Homepage URL for LSM metadata. |
| `id` | string | тАФ | Unique identifier for this makeself config (default: "default"). |
| `ids` | list of string | тАФ | Build IDs filter: only include artifacts whose `id` is in this list. |
| `keywords` | list of string | тАФ | Keywords for LSM metadata. |
| `license` | string | тАФ | License for LSM metadata. |
| `maintainer` | string | тАФ | Maintainer for LSM metadata. |
| `name` | string | тАФ | Display name embedded in the self-extracting archive. |
| `os` | list of string | тАФ | Target OS filter (default: ["linux", "darwin"]). |
| `script` | string | тАФ | Startup script to run when the archive is extracted and executed. Required тАФ the archive will not be created without this. |
| `skip` | StringOrBool | тАФ | Skip this config. Accepts bool or template string. Accepts the legacy `disable:` spelling via serde alias for back-compat with imported configs. |

## `mcp`
MCP server registry publisher configuration.

Publishes an `apiv0.ServerJSON` document to the MCP registry (`https://registry.modelcontextprotocol.io/v0/publish` by default). MCP config (server details flattened onto the publisher block).
| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `auth` | McpAuth | `{"type":"none"}` | Authentication method for the registry's `/v0/publish` endpoint. Defaults to `none` (anonymous publish, allowed for development / staging registries). |
| `description` | string | тАФ | Clear human-readable description of server functionality (max 100 chars). |
